Academy Award for Best Film Editing
infobox.value: is one of the annual awards of the [Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences](/wiki/Academy_of_Motion_Picture_Arts_and_Sciences "Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences") (AMPAS). Nominations for this award are closely correlated with the [Academy Award for Best Picture](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Picture "Academy Award for Best Picture"). For 33 consecutive years, 1981 to 2013, every Best Picture winner had also been nominated for the Film Editing Oscar, and about two thirds of the Best Picture winners have also won for Film Editing.[[1]](#cite_note-Harris-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Only the principal, "[above the line](/wiki/Above-the-line_(filmmaking) "Above-the-line (filmmaking)")" editor(s) as listed in the film's credits are named on the award; additional editors, supervising editors, etc. are not currently eligible.[[3]](#cite_note-Rule13-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Annual award for best film editing

| Academy Award for Best Film Editing | |
| --- | --- |
| Country | [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") |
| Presented by | [Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences](/wiki/Academy_of_Motion_Picture_Arts_and_Sciences "Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences") (AMPAS) |
| First award | February 27, 1935; 91 years ago (1935-02-27) (for films released in [1934](/wiki/1934_in_film "1934 in film")) |
| Most recent winner | [Andy Jurgensen](/wiki/Andy_Jurgensen "Andy Jurgensen") *[One Battle After Another](/wiki/One_Battle_After_Another "One Battle After Another")* ([2025](/wiki/98th_Academy_Awards "98th Academy Awards")) |
| Website | [oscars.org](http://oscars.org) |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/aa/ThelmaSchoonmaker.jpg/250px-ThelmaSchoonmaker.jpg)](/wiki/File:ThelmaSchoonmaker.jpg)

[Thelma Schoonmaker](/wiki/Thelma_Schoonmaker "Thelma Schoonmaker") (left) and Columba Powell (right) at the [2009 Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/2009_Cannes_Film_Festival "2009 Cannes Film Festival"). Schoonmaker is among the deans of film editing; Powell is the son of [Michael Powell](/wiki/Michael_Powell "Michael Powell"), a prominent film director to whom Schoonmaker was married until his death in 1990.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/86/Conrad_A._Nervig_ACE_Headsot.png/250px-Conrad_A._Nervig_ACE_Headsot.png)](/wiki/File:Conrad_A._Nervig_ACE_Headsot.png)

[Conrad A. Nervig](/wiki/Conrad_A._Nervig "Conrad A. Nervig") was the inaugural winner, winning for *[Eskimo](/wiki/Eskimo_(1933_film) "Eskimo (1933 film)")* (1933). He also won for *[King Solomon's Mines](/wiki/King_Solomon%27s_Mines_(1950_film) "King Solomon's Mines (1950 film)")* (1950), alongside [Ralph E. Winters](/wiki/Ralph_E._Winters "Ralph E. Winters").

The **Academy Award for Best Film Editing** is one of the annual awards of the [Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences](/wiki/Academy_of_Motion_Picture_Arts_and_Sciences "Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences") (AMPAS). Nominations for this award are closely correlated with the [Academy Award for Best Picture](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Picture "Academy Award for Best Picture"). For 33 consecutive years, 1981 to 2013, every Best Picture winner had also been nominated for the Film Editing Oscar, and about two thirds of the Best Picture winners have also won for Film Editing.[[1]](#cite_note-Harris-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Only the principal, "[above the line](/wiki/Above-the-line_(filmmaking) "Above-the-line (filmmaking)")" editor(s) as listed in the film's credits are named on the award; additional editors, supervising editors, etc. are not currently eligible.[[3]](#cite_note-Rule13-3)

The nominations for this Academy Award are determined by a ballot of the voting members of the Editing Branch of the academy; there were 220 members of the Editing Branch in 2012.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The members may vote for up to five of the eligible films in the order of their preference; the five films with the largest vote totals are selected as nominees.[[3]](#cite_note-Rule13-3) The Academy Award itself is selected from the nominated films by a subsequent ballot of all active and life members of the academy. This process is essentially the reverse of that of the [British Academy of Film and Television Arts](/wiki/British_Academy_of_Film_and_Television_Arts "British Academy of Film and Television Arts") (BAFTA); nominations for the [BAFTA Award for Best Editing](/wiki/BAFTA_Award_for_Best_Editing "BAFTA Award for Best Editing") are done by a general ballot of academy voters, and the winner is selected by members of the editing chapter.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Academy_Award_for_Best_Film_Editing&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

This award was first given for films released in 1934. The name of this award is occasionally changed; in 2008, it was listed as the **Academy Award for Achievement in Film Editing**.

Four film editors have won this award three times in their career:

* [Ralph Dawson](/wiki/Ralph_Dawson "Ralph Dawson") won for *[A Midsummer Night's Dream](/wiki/A_Midsummer_Night%27s_Dream_(1935_film) "A Midsummer Night's Dream (1935 film)")* (1935), *[Anthony Adverse](/wiki/Anthony_Adverse "Anthony Adverse")* (1936), and *[The Adventures of Robin Hood](/wiki/The_Adventures_of_Robin_Hood "The Adventures of Robin Hood")* (1938)
* [Daniel Mandell](/wiki/Daniel_Mandell "Daniel Mandell") won for *[The Pride of the Yankees](/wiki/The_Pride_of_the_Yankees "The Pride of the Yankees")* (1942), *[The Best Years of Our Lives](/wiki/The_Best_Years_of_Our_Lives "The Best Years of Our Lives")* (1946), and *[The Apartment](/wiki/The_Apartment "The Apartment")* (1960).
* [Michael Kahn](/wiki/Michael_Kahn_(film_editor) "Michael Kahn (film editor)") won for *[Raiders of the Lost Ark](/wiki/Raiders_of_the_Lost_Ark "Raiders of the Lost Ark")* (1981), *[Schindler's List](/wiki/Schindler%27s_List "Schindler's List")* (1993), and *[Saving Private Ryan](/wiki/Saving_Private_Ryan "Saving Private Ryan")* (1998).
* [Thelma Schoonmaker](/wiki/Thelma_Schoonmaker "Thelma Schoonmaker") won for *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")* (1980), *[The Aviator](/wiki/The_Aviator_(2004_film) "The Aviator (2004 film)")* (2004), and *[The Departed](/wiki/The_Departed "The Departed")* (2006).

To date, three film directors have won this award: [James Cameron](/wiki/James_Cameron "James Cameron"), [Alfonso Cuarón](/wiki/Alfonso_Cuar%C3%B3n "Alfonso Cuarón"), and [Sean Baker](/wiki/Sean_Baker "Sean Baker"), for the films *[Titanic](/wiki/Titanic_(1997_film) "Titanic (1997 film)")*, *[Gravity](/wiki/Gravity_(2013_film) "Gravity (2013 film)")*, and *[Anora](/wiki/Anora_(2024_film) "Anora (2024 film)")*, respectively. Directors [David Lean](/wiki/David_Lean "David Lean"), [Steve James](/wiki/Steve_James_(producer) "Steve James (producer)"), [Joel Coen and Ethan Coen](/wiki/The_Coen_brothers "The Coen brothers") (under the alias "Roderick Jaynes"), [Michel Hazanavicius](/wiki/Michel_Hazanavicius "Michel Hazanavicius"), [Jean-Marc Vallée](/wiki/Jean-Marc_Vall%C3%A9e "Jean-Marc Vallée") (under the alias "John Mac McMurphy"), [Chloé Zhao](/wiki/Chlo%C3%A9_Zhao "Chloé Zhao"), and [Josh Safdie](/wiki/Josh_Safdie "Josh Safdie") have been nominated for editing their own films as well, with Cameron, Cuarón, and the Coens each being nominated for the award twice. Also, Best Film Editing winner, [Walter Murch](/wiki/Walter_Murch "Walter Murch"), although known for film editing and sound, directed the Oscar nominated *[Return to Oz](/wiki/Return_to_Oz "Return to Oz")*, and is, to date, the only person with Oscars for both sound engineering and film editing—winning them in the same year for his work on *[The English Patient](/wiki/The_English_Patient_(film) "The English Patient (film)")*.

Additionally, former film editors [Robert Wise](/wiki/Robert_Wise "Robert Wise") (nominee for *[Citizen Kane](/wiki/Citizen_Kane "Citizen Kane")*), [Hal Ashby](/wiki/Hal_Ashby "Hal Ashby") (winner for *[In the Heat of the Night](/wiki/In_the_Heat_of_the_Night_(film) "In the Heat of the Night (film)")*), and [Francis D. Lyon](/wiki/Francis_D._Lyon "Francis D. Lyon") (co-winner for *[Body and Soul](/wiki/Body_and_Soul_(1947_film) "Body and Soul (1947 film)")*) became directors whose films were subsequently nominated for Best Film Editing themselves. These films include *[Somebody Up There Likes Me](/wiki/Somebody_Up_There_Likes_Me_(1956_film) "Somebody Up There Likes Me (1956 film)")*, *[I Want to Live!](/wiki/I_Want_to_Live! "I Want to Live!")*, *[West Side Story](/wiki/West_Side_Story_(1961_film) "West Side Story (1961 film)")*, *[The Sound of Music](/wiki/The_Sound_of_Music_(film) "The Sound of Music (film)")*, *[The Sand Pebbles](/wiki/The_Sand_Pebbles_(film) "The Sand Pebbles (film)")*, and *[The Andromeda Strain](/wiki/The_Andromeda_Strain_(film) "The Andromeda Strain (film)")* for Wise; *[Bound for Glory](/wiki/Bound_for_Glory_(1976_film) "Bound for Glory (1976 film)")* and *[Coming Home](/wiki/Coming_Home_(1978_film) "Coming Home (1978 film)")* for Ashby; and *[Crazylegs](/wiki/Crazylegs_(film) "Crazylegs (film)")* for Lyon.
